subheading: Generate clean anime art and characters from a text prompt using open, editable ComfyUI workflows built on the Anima, Neta Lumina, and NewBie models.
extendedDescription:
What people actually want from an ai anime generator is a result that looks like real anime, not a smudged approximation with melted hands and a face that drifts between every render. Most consumer text to anime sites lean on one locked checkpoint and a hidden prompt, so you get a single house style and no way to steer it. The ComfyUI workflows on this page run dedicated anime models with every setting exposed, so you write the prompt, pick the look, and keep iterating until the character and the style are the ones you meant.

The cluster is built around open-weight anime models, each tuned for a different job. Anima, a 2B-parameter text-to-image model from CircleStone Labs trained on several million anime images, handles fast, expressive illustration and concept art, and ships as both a base and a preview workflow. Neta Lumina, a fine-tune of Lumina-Image-2.0 released under an Apache 2.0 license, reads long natural-language prompts in English, Japanese, and Chinese, which makes it strong for detailed character concept design and posters. NewBie Exp0.1 is a 3.5B Next-DiT model that uses structured Danbooru-style tags for precise control over a character's appearance, clothing, and pose in multi-figure scenes.

Because the whole graph is open, you control the parts a one-click app hides. Anima renders between 512 and 1536 pixels, so you set the resolution for a wallpaper, a print, or a square avatar. You write the positive and negative prompts yourself, fix the seed to lock a character and re-roll only the background, and adjust the sampler and step count to trade speed for detail. Want a different aesthetic on the same prompt? Swap the checkpoint and run it again, the difference between an anime art generator you own and a slider you rent.

Everything runs from editable workflows, locally in ComfyUI on your own GPU or on Comfy Cloud with no install. The images come out with no watermark and there is no cap on how many you make, so a character sheet of twenty poses costs nothing per image beyond the compute. These are mainstream, clean anime workflows for illustration, character design, and manga-style art, so an original character, a Ghibli-flavored landscape, or a chibi sticker set all come from the same stack with full control of every step.

styles.title: Original character design
styles.description: Spell out hair, eyes, outfit, and expression to design an OC, then fix the seed and re-roll backgrounds around the same face.

styles.title: Ghibli-style scenery
styles.description: Prompt soft, painterly landscapes and slice-of-life scenes that read as hand-drawn cel animation rather than photo filters.

styles.title: Shonen and action art
styles.description: Generate dynamic poses, speed lines, and dramatic lighting for battle scenes using NewBie's tag-precise character control.

styles.title: Chibi and sticker sets
styles.description: Render compact chibi versions of a character at small resolutions for emotes, stickers, and avatar packs.

styles.title: Manga and lineart
styles.description: Produce high-contrast black-and-white panels and cel-shaded inks for a manga art generator look without flat color.

styles.title: Poster and key visual
styles.description: Use Neta Lumina's long-prompt comprehension to compose a detailed poster or key visual at full print resolution.

howToUse: Pick the model that fits your goal: Anima for fast illustration, Neta Lumina for long prompts, or NewBie for tag-precise characters.
Open the workflow on Comfy Cloud with no install, or load it in your local ComfyUI.
Write a positive prompt describing the character, style, and scene, then add a negative prompt to keep out unwanted artifacts.
Set the resolution between 512 and 1536 pixels and choose a portrait, square, or wide aspect for your output.
Run the workflow, then fix the seed on a result you like and re-roll only the background or pose to keep the character consistent.
Export the version you want at full resolution with no watermark, and swap the checkpoint to try a different anime style on the same prompt.

faqItems.question: What is the best ai anime generator workflow to start with?
faqItems.answer: For most people the Anima base workflow is the easiest entry point, since it is fast, trained on millions of anime images, and handles general illustration well. If you write long, descriptive prompts, Neta Lumina reads them more closely, and NewBie is the pick for tag-precise multi-character scenes. All three are open models you can run and tune in ComfyUI.

faqItems.question: How do you generate anime art with AI from just a text prompt?
faqItems.answer: You type a positive prompt describing the character, outfit, and scene, add a negative prompt for anything you want to avoid, and run the workflow. The model turns that text into an anime image, which you can refine by editing the prompt or fixing the seed. No reference photo is needed, since these are pure text to anime workflows.

faqItems.question: Can AI draw anime characters and keep them consistent across poses?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Once you generate a character you like, fix the seed so the model reuses the same starting noise, then change only the pose or background prompt. NewBie's structured tag prompts also help bind appearance details to one figure in multi-character scenes. This is how you build a character sheet that stays on-model.

faqItems.question: What anime styles can this anime art generator produce?
faqItems.answer: You drive the style through the prompt, so Ghibli-flavored scenery, shonen action, chibi, cel-shaded, and manga lineart all work. Swapping the checkpoint between Anima, Neta Lumina, and NewBie shifts the underlying aesthetic further. Running one prompt through several models is a quick way to compare looks.

faqItems.question: Does this work as a manga art generator for black-and-white pages?
faqItems.answer: It does. You can prompt high-contrast lineart and cel-shaded inks to get a manga panel look rather than soft full color. Combining that with a fixed character seed keeps your protagonist recognizable from panel to panel. Export each frame at full resolution for layout.

faqItems.question: Can I run these anime workflows locally or do I need the cloud?
faqItems.answer: Both work. You can run them on your own GPU in ComfyUI, where Anima fits comfortably on consumer cards thanks to its 2B size, or open them on Comfy Cloud with no install if your machine is light on VRAM. The workflow is identical either way, so you can move between local and cloud as needed.

faqItems.question: How much does it cost to use this anime character generator?
faqItems.answer: Running the workflows locally uses your own GPU, so there is no per-image charge once the model is downloaded. Comfy Cloud is a paid plan with tiered seats if you would rather not install anything or lack a strong GPU. Either way the output has no watermark and there is no cap on how many images you make.

faqItems.question: Is the output watermarked or limited like a typical anime ai art generator?
faqItems.answer: No. The images you generate carry no watermark and there is no usage cap built into the workflow. Because the models ship under open weights, you also keep full control of the prompt, seed, and settings, which most hosted anime style ai image generator sites hide from you.
